articles.title: 'Beware of pickpockets': Rahul Gandhi targets CBSE over re-evaluation fee amid OSM row
articles.description: CBSE fees: Rahul Gandhi attacks the CBSE re-evaluation fee structure, saying students pay to correct alleged board errors. He cites charges for scanned copies, re-totalling and review amid the OSM controversy, and seeks accountability in education.

articles.title: Pro-Trump Outsider Wins Colombia Vote, Setting Up Heated Runoff
articles.description: De la Espriella had 43.7% of the vote with nearly all ballots counted, according to official results.

articles.title: After stating he want burst, Jammu and Kashmir CM Omar Abdullah convenes crucial legislative party meeting
articles.description: As the political landscape shifts, Jammu and Kashmir's Chief Minister Omar Abdullah is set to convene with National Conference lawmakers on June 3. The agenda is anticipated to delve into pressing governance issues, the long-awaited appointment of an advocate general, and ongoing debates surrounding reservation policies.

articles.title: KTR dares CM Reddy: Fulfill 420 promises before seeking re-election
articles.description: BRS leader KTR challenged CM Revanth Reddy to fulfill all 420 poll promises and six guarantees before contesting elections again. He accused the Congress government of planning to end the 24-hour free power supply for Telangana farmers.

articles.title: India holds third spot in global Schengen visa demand, rejection rate rises to 15.8%
articles.description: At the country level, Slovenia recorded the highest rejection rate for Indian applicants at 46.5%.

articles.title: Zohran Mamdani Becomes First NYC Mayor In 62 Years To Skip Israel Day Parade
articles.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani broke tradition by not attending the annual Israel Day Parade, a customary event for political leaders, due to his support for Palestinian rights. His absence drew criticism from opponents.

articles.title: US proposes 'gradual de-escalation' plan between Israel and Hezbollah
articles.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio held separate talks with Lebanese President Joseph Aoun and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu as Washington seeks to ease tensions between Israel and Lebanon, a US official said on Sunday.

articles.title: Bengal's Biggest Power Show Yet: 35 Ministers To Join Suvendu Adhikari's Cabinet Today
articles.description: The West Bengal government plans a significant cabinet expansion, with 35 new ministers expected to take the oath, increasing the Council of Ministers to 40. Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari announced the ceremony will take place at 11 am at Lok Bhavan, attended by the Governor.

articles.title: US targets Iranian radar, drone sites after Tehran downs American drone
articles.description: Iran's paramilitary Revolutionary Guard in a statement carried by the state-run IRNA news agency said that US forces had targeted a telecommunications tower on an island

articles.title: Criminal activities of Lawrence Bishnoi gang in Canada being driven by at least 3 factions: Police official
articles.description: This was revealed by a Canadian police officer while testifying before the Immigration and Refugee Board of Canada. The testimony came from Kevin St Louis, an Edmonton Police Service official and an investigating officer for Project AI-Extortion

articles.title: BJP doesn't back violence, says Gaurav Vallabh on Abhishek attack
articles.description: BJP's Gaurav Vallabh denies backing violence after TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee was attacked in Sonarpur. He claims TMC's own workers are rejecting leaders. Abhishek Banerjee has alleged the attack was 'BJP-sponsored'. Five people have been arrested.

articles.title: NEET UG 2026: IYC protests in Pune, demands action against Pradhan
articles.description: Indian Youth Congress members protested in Pune over the NEET UG 2026 paper leak, calling for action against Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. IYC President Uday Bhanu Chib vowed to continue protests until justice is served to impacted youth.

articles.title: JD(S) awaits BJP's call on renominating H D Gowda to Rajya Sabha
articles.description: JD(S) awaits BJP's call on renominating Gowda to RS

articles.title: Prince Andrew Faces Fresh Accusations Over 'Incident With Woman' Amid Epstein Allegations
articles.description: Prince Andrew is under renewed investigation regarding allegations of inappropriate behavior towards a woman during the 2002 Royal Ascot event.

articles.title: Trump runs away, Putin now ready? NATO heading towards dangerous security vacuum amid Iran crisis
articles.description: Fresh questions are being raised over Donald Trump’s Europe strategy after the attack on Europe’s biggest nuclear plant. Reports of a U.S. military drawdown across the continent have sparked concern among NATO allies, with fears that any weakening of Western deterrence could hand Vladimir Putin a strategic opening.

articles.title: Mike Pence Sounds Alarm on Trump and JD Vance: 'Republicans Have Lost Way'
articles.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence criticized the Republican Party's shift under Donald Trump during his recent television appearances. He expressed concern that 'core' conservative principles, such as limited government and free market economics, have been abandoned.

articles.title: Trump's surprised 'we shouldn't have been...' Iran remarks come as Mojtaba refuses nuclear deal?
articles.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has sparked fresh debate with remarks on Iran, defending the 2025 U.S. strikes while also warning against the long-term consequences of military interventions. Trump argued that B-2 bomber attacks prevented Iran from acquiring a nuclear weapon and claimed Washington deliberately avoided destroying much of Iran's military infrastructure. He also suggested that broader diplomacy could help reopen the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz and reduce regional tensions. The comments have fueled speculation about a possible shift in Trump's Middle East approach as discussions over regional security, Iran and future peace efforts continue to dominate global attention.

articles.title: Corruption crackdown: Rajasthan government dismisses 20 officials, suspends 332
articles.description: Rajasthan government dismisses 20 officials and suspends 332 in a major crackdown on corruption and administrative negligence.

articles.title: Israel Erupts: Protesters Turn Netanyahu Into Trump's 'Puppet' in Street Spectacle | Watch
articles.description: Thousands of Israelis took to the streets of Jerusalem and Tel Aviv in fresh protests against Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's government. One viral moment showed a protester wearing a Donald Trump mask while carrying a baby-like Netanyahu doll, sparking intense reactions online. Demonstrators called for political change, early elections, and a new approach to regional security, while accusing the government of failing the public. The protests come as Israel faces a deepening political crisis and growing debate over the country's future direction.

articles.title: What did Iran mean by 'new phase of war'? Mojtaba’s top negotiator reveals battle plan to counter US
articles.description: Iran’s Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf has declared that a “new phase” in the confrontation with the United States and Israel has begun, as diplomatic talks remain stalled. Speaking during a parliamentary session, Ghalibaf emphasized that Iran will not approve any agreement unless it secures what he described as the “rights of the Iranian nation.” He warned against trusting enemy promises and framed the current situation as a broader struggle spanning military, diplomatic, economic, and social fronts. The statement comes amid rising regional tensions and renewed debate inside Iran over how to respond to external pressure and ongoing geopolitical disputes.

articles.title: Madhya Pradesh govt to give farmers helmets; will save lives in road accidents, says CM Yadav
articles.description: Madhya Pradesh News: CM Mohan Yadav announces the MP government will give helmets to farmers riding motorcycles to sell produce, aiming to prevent road accidents.

articles.title: China This Week | EU’s ‘Chemotherapy’ for battling trade surplus, changes in household system, and new nuclear test site photos
articles.description: Every week, we recap highlights of the news from China. This edition looks at the EU’s response to growing trade friction, the “hukou” system and reports of construction activities at the Lop Nur nuclear testing site.

articles.title: Special Intensive Revision of Electoral Rolls Begins in Four States
articles.description: The Enumeration Phase of the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls has begun in Odisha, Mizoram, Sikkim, and Manipur. The ECI has set a deadline of June 28 for electors to submit forms for inclusion in the draft electoral rolls.

articles.title: PM Modi is the only world PM who talks to citizens monthly: Malhotra
articles.description: BJP's Harsh Malhotra lauded PM Modi after the 134th 'Mann ki Baat', calling him the only world PM to address citizens monthly. In the broadcast, Modi praised athletes like Gurindervir Singh for setting new records at the National Athletics Cup.

articles.title: Rahul Gandhi slams govt over CBSE row, alleges students were targeted
articles.description: Rahul Gandhi attacked the BJP govt over CBSE exam evaluation issues, alleging students were targeted. He highlighted tender irregularities and accused PM Modi of complicity, while CBSE deployed cybersecurity experts to fix vulnerabilities.

articles.title: Himanta Biswa Sarma, BJP workers in Assam listen to Mann Ki Baat
articles.description: Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma and lakhs of BJP workers listened to the 134th Mann Ki Baat. PM Modi spoke on summer heat, mangoes, the return of Chola-era copper plates from the Netherlands, and India's rich history of astronomy.

articles.title: Kejriwal opposes Lodha mega project in Goa, alleges environmental damage
articles.description: AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal opposed the Lodha mega project in Goa's Karapur, citing environmental damage and corruption. He backed protesting villagers and vowed to scrap projects approved under Section 39A if AAP is voted to power in the state.

articles.title: Odisha Governor appoints IPS Ananya Awasthi as first female ADC
articles.description: IPS officer Ananya Awasthi has been appointed as the first woman Aide-de-Camp (ADC) to Odisha Governor Dr Hari Babu Kambhampati. Previously serving as ACP in Cuttack, her appointment is hailed as a significant milestone for women in public service.

articles.title: Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari's First Cabinet Expansion Tomorrow: 35 Ministers to Take Oath
articles.description: West Bengal's Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari will expand his cabinet on Monday, with 35 ministers set to take the oath of office at 11 AM, administered by Governor RN Ravi.

articles.title: Kerala govt. announces Ministers in charge of districts
articles.description: Kerala government announces Cabinet Ministers assigned to each district for effective governance.

articles.title: NATO claims new drone incident but officials find no proof: Putin catches west's lie about 'attack'?
articles.description: NATO is on edge after a fresh drone scare forced Germany to briefly shut down Munich Airport, its second‐busiest hub, just one day after a Russian‐made drone slammed into a residential building in Romania. The incident reignited fears about airspace security and the spillover of the Ukraine war deep into alliance territory. Is this the new normal for Europe’s skies — and how close is the alliance to a serious miscalculation?

articles.title: Did Xi Jinping betray Trump? US rival accused of helping Iran shoot down America's $90 million F-15
articles.description: Fresh reports are raising new questions over China’s alleged role in Iran’s military buildup after claims emerged that Chinese systems may have helped track and target a U.S. F-15 during the Iran conflict. The revelations come as U.S.-China tensions flare over Taiwan, with sharp exchanges between Washington and Beijing adding another layer to an already volatile geopolitical standoff.

articles.title: 'A chat with Soros agents': Rahul's dig at BJP as he meets CBSE students
articles.description: Rahul Gandhi met CBSE Class 12 students labelled 'anti-national' after questioning an incorrect Physics answer sheet. The board later admitted an error. Gandhi criticized the government's response, stating the students deserve a secure future and that problems can't be solved by denying them. The incident highlighted scrutiny of the board's evaluation process.

articles.title: As Jarange-Patil ends fast, government cautious on OBC quota and Satara Gazette
articles.description: Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis says the government has to ensure that any reservation granted to the Marathas will withstand constitutional and legal scrutiny.

articles.title: EU weighs temporary freeze on Russia oil price cap over Iran war: Report
articles.description: Heads up, there's an urgent development regarding EU-Russia oil! According to Bloomberg News, the European Union is reportedly considering a temporary pause on the price cap for Russian oil. This potential move is apparently being discussed in light of the ongoing conflict in Iran, suggesting a shift in strategic priorities.

articles.title: ‘I defended my country, now I am a target’: Abhishek Banerjee slams BJP after Sonarpur attack
articles.description: TMC leader Abhishek Banerjee thanks Rahul Gandhi for his support after the Sonarpur attack, alleging political violence and state-sponsored terrorism while accusing the BJP of targeting opponents.

articles.title: 'INDIA Stands United': Abhishek Banerjee Thanks Rahul Gandhi For 'Constant Support' After Attack
articles.description: This comes after Rahul Gandhi had condemned the attack on the Trinamool Congress MP and called for action against those responsible.

articles.title: Experts Explain | Can Election Commission decide who is an Indian citizen? What Supreme Court said in SIR verdict
articles.description: It is worth noting that the judgment itself does not completely separate removal from the electoral roll from the loss of citizenship. With the legal battle over, the practical burden of avoiding disenfranchisement shifts to citizens.

articles.title: BJP alleges Mamata Banerjee pressured private hospital to admit nephew Abhishek
articles.description: Mamata Banerjee: BJP claimed Mamata Banerjee pressured a Kolkata private hospital to admit TMC MP Abhishek Banerjee despite doctors finding no major injuries. The allegation follows an attack on the Diamond Harbour MP during a visit to violence-hit families.

articles.title: India's new CDS Subramani takes the helm, armed forces face transformation test with 'Jai' vision
articles.description: India’s new Chief of Defence Staff, General N. S. Raja Subramani, has taken charge with a strong emphasis on transformation, jointness, and self-reliance. His early messaging points to a reform-driven approach centered on integration across the services, faster innovation, and deeper indigenisation as India pushes to modernize its military structure.

articles.title: AISA Calls June 1 Protest at Education Ministry, Demands Dharmendra Pradhan's Resignation
articles.description: The All India Students Association (AISA) has called a protest outside the Education Ministry on June 1, demanding accountability over NEET, CBSE and CUET-related issues.

articles.title: Just When Iran Deal Seemed Done, Trump Sends It Back for Revisions: Here's Why
articles.description: Trump's main concern relates to Iran's enriched uranium stockpile. The current draft does not contain detailed concessions beyond that pledge.

articles.title: UP Police Constable admit card 2026 likely to be released tomorrow at upprpb.in: Check steps to download hall ticket
articles.description: UP Police Constable admit cards for 2026 are anticipated tomorrow, June 1, on upprpb.in. The offline OMR-based exam is scheduled for June 8-10 across Uttar Pradesh. Candidates can expect city intimation details beforehand. Ensure you carry the admit card and valid ID to the exam centre. Stay tuned to the official website for updates.

articles.title: We’re close to a ‘very good deal’: Trump on talks with Iran to extend ceasefire, reopen Strait of Hormuz
articles.description: Trump also shared that he hoped to avoid military escalation if a satisfactory deal was reached with Iran.

articles.title: One third of coaches are egoistic; stubborn and abusive, says Sumit Antil
articles.description: Sumit Antil reveals one-third of coaches are egoistic, stubborn, and abusive, sharing his own experiences as a victim.

articles.title: 'He can take Abhishek to Hyderabad or anywhere else': Mamata on Rahul Gandhi's outreach
articles.description: Mamata said Rahul Gandhi had called her to express concern over the incident and offered assistance for Abhishek Banerjee’s treatment if required.

articles.title: Footage of Surya case goes viral, sparks outrage
articles.description: Footage of Surya case goes viral, sparks outrage

articles.title: What Is Iran's New '27 Rajab' Vessel? All About The IRGC Fast Attack Boat
articles.description: Donald Trump has delivered a strikingly contradictory message on the Iran war, saying the United States "shouldn't have been in Iran" while simultaneously defending American military strikes against Tehran.

articles.title: Indian Navy Confined Pakistani Fleet to Its Ports, Crippled Maritime Trade During Operation Sindoor
articles.description: India’s top defence leadership on Sunday highlighted the decisive impact of the Indian Navy during 'Operation Sindoor'.

articles.title: IPL Final 2026: Bengaluru Police Bans Public Celebrations, Bike Rallies
articles.description: The Bengaluru police states that public shall not engage in any kind of celebrations in public places after the result of the final match is announced.
